Level0: No career stages or progression pathway defined for teachers
Level1: Generic promotion levels exist but not based on professional competence
Level2: Some progression levels defined but not aligned to the 4-stage continental model
Level3: Four stages mentioned (Beginner, Proficient, Expert, Distinguished) but lack clear descriptors or requirements
Level4: Four stages defined with competence descriptors for each stage but missing progression criteria
Level5: Fully defined 4-stage career path (Beginner→Proficient→Expert→Distinguished) with detailed competences, CPD requirements, and progression criteria for each stage

Level0: No professional standards or competences defined for teachers
Level1: Generic teaching guidelines exist but not structured as professional standards
Level2: Some professional domains covered but incomplete (missing 2-3 domains from the 5-domain framework)
Level3: All 5 domains mentioned (Knowledge, Skills, Values, Partnerships, Leadership) but lack detailed competences/descriptors
Level4: All 5 domains with substantial competences defined but partial alignment to AFSCTP
Level5: Full alignment: All 5 domains (Professional Knowledge, Skills, Values/Attributes, Partnerships, Leadership) with comprehensive competences matching AFSCTP

Level0: No standards defined for school leaders/principals
Level2: Some leadership standards exist but cover fewer than 4 of the 7 key areas
Level3: Leadership standards exist but not aligned to the 7-standard continental framework
Level4: Substantial alignment with 7 standards but missing detailed competences for career stages
Level5: Full alignment with all 7 leadership standards: Developing Self/Others, Leading Knowledge/Practice, Managing Resources, Promoting Improvement, Generating Resources, Supporting Learners, and Community Engagement

Level0: No competency assessment or testing mentioned
Level1: Informal/self-assessment only with no standardized testing
Level2: Assessment exists but not explicitly linked to career stage progression
Level3: Competency testing mentioned for some transitions but not standardized across all stages
Level4: Structured competency tests for progression but lacking technology-based implementation (CBT)
Level5: Comprehensive competency testing for all career transitions (Beginner→Proficient→Expert→Distinguished) using standardized computer-based testing aligned to AFSCTP

Level0: No reference to African Union education strategies or international frameworks
Level1: Generic reference to African education without specific strategy alignment
Level2: Mentions CESA 16-25 but not integrated into the standards framework
Level3: References CESA 16-25 and Agenda 2063 but lacks explicit implementation pathways
Level4: Substantial alignment with CESA 16-25 Strategic Objective 1 and continental teacher policies
Level5: Explicit alignment with CESA 16-25, Agenda 2063, SDG4c, and Continental Teacher Mobility Protocol with clear implementation strategies
